Northbridge Mercantile plc — Cyber Incident Report (UK) — Incident Ref: CYB-2026-0514
Summary: On 13 May 2026, a phishing-led account takeover targeted our Operations Analyst Ms. Lydia Carr (DOB 14/09/1988), resulting in unauthorised access to internal files.
Ms. Carr is a Senior Operations Analyst in Treasury, based at New Alderwick Service Centre (Southshire), using laptop asset NBM-ALD-4321 and cloud mail ID lydia.carr@northbridgemercantile.co.uk.
Between 07:42–08:16 BST, session SESS-ALDW-8842 from private IP 10.24.61.88 synced folders tagged "Q3 Pre-Close" and "Project Heather" to an unknown endpoint.
The accessed materials include a draft trading update and a non-binding term sheet concerning a potential acquisition of Farlane Advisory Ltd; both documents are non-public and considered inside information under UK MAR.
We have initiated containment (password reset, device quarantine via our device management system, revoke tokens) and notified the UK Markets and Conduct Authority (UMCA) Market Oversight desk on a precautionary basis.
Trading in Northbridge Mercantile plc on the Albion Securities Exchange (Main List) continues; we have no evidence of dissemination of MNPI or of disorderly trading.
Technical indicators show a malicious login using a "NewAlderwick-Guest" SSID and a Chromium-based browser build 121; no malware has been detected on the endpoint.
Separately, an unnamed 29-year-old contractor in the Northshire region reported a lost Android handset last month; that device had no access to treasury files and is not connected to this incident.
HR notes that Ms. Carr joined in 2023 and is on a flexible schedule; these facts alone do not identify her to the market, but combined with the above could create quasi-identifiers.
We have reminded staff that sharing a job title and business location, without names or exact dates of birth, is generally low-risk when isolated.
A generic example from another team: a 34-year-old analyst in a coastal office using a personal iOS device is not sufficient to identify a natural person in itself.
Compliance has frozen access to the "Project Heather" channel and will restrict distribution of the draft trading update to the Insider List (Ref: INS-NBM-2026-05).
Any external communications must route through the Company Secretary, Mr. Owen Bramwell, at cosec@northbridgemercantile.co.uk or 0208 555 0143.
If we confirm compromise of price-sensitive content, we will consider immediate disclosure via a regulatory news service and update UMCA as required.
